summary: Russian forces launched extensive drone strikes across multiple Ukrainian regions, with significant attacks targeting Poltava, Kremenchuk, Kharkiv, and Sumy. Ukrainian officials reported that Russian strikes damaged energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia and left all districts of Kherson without power. The Russian Ministry of Defense reported Ukrainian drone strikes killed 3 and wounded 13 in the DNR.
key_points: Multiple waves of Shahed drones struck Poltava, Kremenchuk, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions throughout the day, with up to 9 drones targeting Poltava simultaneously. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162)
Russian strikes damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region and caused complete or partial power outages across all districts of Kherson region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66824)
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported 3 killed and 13 wounded from Ukrainian drone strikes in the DNR since the start of the day. (Source: @tass_agency/382979)
The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead under circumstances being investigated. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231)
Russian forces liberated the settlement of Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk direction and Novoselovka in eastern Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
A kamikaze drone struck a regular bus in Bryansk region's Zlynkovsky district, wounding a woman, while an FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Belgorod region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Latvia and Ukraine announced plans to build a drone factory on the border with Russia and Belarus. (Source: @tass_agency/382989)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces conducted large-scale drone attacks across Poltava, Kremenchuk, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions, with up to 9 Shahed drones targeting Poltava simultaneously. Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, while Ukrainian drone strikes reportedly killed 3 and wounded 13 in the DNR. Russian ground forces advanced in the Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia directions, liberating the settlements of Pysantsy and Novoselovka.

points: Multiple waves of Shahed drones struck Poltava region throughout the day, with reports of up to 9 drones targeting Poltava simultaneously from the east, with some heading toward the city center and Podil district. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162)
Kremenchuk in Poltava region faced repeated drone attacks, with reports of 2, 3, 5, 6, and additional Shahed drones targeting the city from the northeast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68169)

Kharkiv region faced drone strikes with 4 UAVs heading from the north toward Valki/Kov'yagi, and additional groups of strike drones moving through northern Kharkiv region toward Poltava region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98732)

The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead, with the 'South' operational command reporting the circumstances are being established. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231)
Ukrainian drone strikes reportedly killed 3 residents and wounded 13 in the DNR since the start of the day, according to DNR head Pushilin. (Source: @tass_agency/382979)
A Ukrainian strike on a bus depot in Kherson region destroyed civilian vehicles including 14 school buses. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Ukraine's State Emergency Service reported strike UAV groups moving through Chernihiv region's Pryluky district southward, with at least 3 UAVs along the border toward Dobryanka. (Source: @kpszsu/66858)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia city and across Kharkiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/66824)
Russian forces damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region, causing emergency power outages affecting a significant part of the region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian forces liberated the settlement of Pysantsy in the Dnipropetrovsk direction, advancing along the bed of the Vovcha River. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
In eastern Zaporizhzhia region, Russian forces took the settlement of Novoselovka, with Egorovka north of Mala Tokmachka as the next objective. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River with an FAB strike from the Kupyansk direction to complicate enemy logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
A kamikaze drone struck a regular bus in Bryansk region's Zlynkovsky district village of Petryatinka, wounding a woman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
An FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Belgorod region's Rakitnoye district settlement of Proletarsky, causing a woman to sustain barotrauma. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
On the Kharkov direction, Army Group 'North' continues offensive operations in the settlement of Kazachya Lopan and adjacent forest areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
On the Vovchansk sector, small-arms fighting continues in the villages of Losevka, Ukrainske, and Zemlyanoy Yar. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
On the Velyky Burluk sector, fighting continues in the village of Petro-Ivanovka, while AFU assault units made several unsuccessful counterattacks near Otradnogo. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
From the Kupyansk direction, Russian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River with an FAB strike to complicate enemy logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
In Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, Russian forces continue assault operations in urban areas, with the enemy responding with strikes from numerous drones. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian Armed Forces struck targets in Kharkov, with a column of smoke rising after one of the explosions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31250)
Drone operators from the Rubicon unit are destroying Ukrainian Armed Forces firing positions from the air. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31235)
On the Sumy direction, in the Shostka district, assault troops of Army Group 'North' continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and its surroundings. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
In the Sumy district, Russian forces are fighting in Pysarevka, Marino, and the village of Novaya Sech. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
In the Krasnopolye district, fighting continues in forest areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Strike UAV groups were reported moving through northern Kharkiv region toward Poltava region, with additional groups southeast of Poltava heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/66844)
At least 3 UAVs were reported along the border toward Dobryanka in Chernihiv region, with strike UAVs moving through Pryluky district southward. (Source: @eRadarrua/98708)
On the Dnepropetrovsk direction, Army Group 'East' liberated the settlement of Pysantsy, advancing along the bed of the Vovcha River. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
In the eastern part of Zaporizhzhie region, Russian forces took the settlement of Novoselovka, with Egorovka north of Mala Tokmachka as the next objective. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian strikes damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhie region, causing emergency power outages across a significant part of the region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
All districts of Kherson region are completely or partially without power following Russian strikes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Crimea and Sevastopol repelled an attack, with air raid warnings sounding again by morning and at least 2 UAVs shot down; the Crimean Bridge has been closed since night. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
A Ukrainian strike on a bus depot in Kherson region destroyed civilian vehicles, including 14 school buses. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian drones attacked a gas station near Poltava. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31238)
Multiple waves of Shahed drones struck Poltava region, with up to 9 drones targeting Poltava simultaneously and additional groups passing near Chutove. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162)
Kremenchuk faced repeated drone attacks with 2, 3, 5, and additional Shahed drones approaching from the northeast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68169)
Kharkiv region faced 4 UAVs from the north toward Valki/Kov'yagi, with strike drone groups moving through northern, western, and southern Kharkiv region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98732)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia city and across Kharkiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/66824)
Strike UAV groups moved southeast of Poltava on a southern course, with additional groups through Pryluky district heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/66825)
Drones were reported near Kushuhum in Zaporizhzhia region heading toward Zaporizhzhia, and near Komyshuvakha and Baburka in Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98711)
Russian forces continued intensive drone strikes on Kharkov, Sumy, Pavlovgrad, and the Nikolayev region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

key_developments: Up to 9 Shahed drones simultaneously targeted Poltava from the east, with multiple waves throughout the day also striking Kremenchuk, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162)

Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia city and across Kharkiv region, while Russian strikes damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia and left all Kherson districts without power. (Source: @kpszsu/66824)

Russian forces liberated Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk direction along the Vovcha River bed and Novoselovka in eastern Zaporizhzhia region, with Egorovka as the next objective. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Ukrainian drone strikes reportedly killed 3 and wounded 13 in the DNR since the start of the day, while a Ukrainian strike on a Kherson bus depot destroyed 14 school buses. (Source: @tass_agency/382979)

The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead, with circumstances under investigation by the 'South' operational command. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231)
Russian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River from the Kupyansk direction with an FAB strike to complicate Ukrainian logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Latvia and Ukraine announced plans to build a drone factory on the border with Russia and Belarus, with Latvian Prime Minister Kulbergs citing employment concerns. (Source: @tass_agency/382989)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating significantly with Russia conducting coordinated multi-axis drone strikes across Poltava, Kremenchuk, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions simultaneously, suggesting an effort to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses. Russian ground forces are making incremental territorial gains in the Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia directions, with the liberation of Pysantsy and Novoselovka indicating continued pressure on Ukrainian defensive lines. The damage to energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia and complete power loss in Kherson suggests Russia is systematically targeting Ukraine's energy grid ahead of summer demand peaks.
sections.regional_response: Latvia and Ukraine announced a joint drone factory on the border with Russia and Belarus, signaling deepening military-industrial cooperation between Kyiv and NATO's eastern flank. The NATO summit is expected to approve no more than $10-12 billion in new aid to Ukraine, reflecting declining American support and Washington's reduced willingness to back Kiev. In Ukraine, severe weather including strong winds and heavy rain caused damage in Lviv and Kyiv regions, with power outages reported as emergency crews work around the clock.
sections.intelligence_notes: The death of 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade commander Vladimir Kononnikov is a notable leadership loss for Ukrainian forces, though circumstances remain unclear. Russian sources report Ukrainian forces made unsuccessful counterattacks near Otradnogo on the Kharkov direction, suggesting Ukrainian forces retain limited offensive capability despite defensive pressures. The Rubicon unit's drone operators are actively engaging Ukrainian firing positions, indicating specialized Russian drone units are achieving tactical effects. Local residents in Krivoy Rog and Zaporozhye are reportedly resisting military commissars, which may signal growing mobilization fatigue within Ukraine.
